chief_complaint: One high sugar reading this morning.
history_of_present_illness: Ms. Delgado got a fasting glucose of 247 this morning, higher than her usual 120-150. She notes she started a strict low-carb (keto) diet about a week ago on her doctor's encouragement and yesterday was a celebration where she 'had a big slice of birthday cake and some juice.' She also started a new diabetes pill two weeks ago. She feels completely well, no symptoms.

review_of_systems.constitutional: Feels well, no excessive thirst or urination today.
review_of_systems.endocrine: Denies blurred vision, no recent infections.

social_history.living_situation: Lives with husband and two children

medication_adherence.detail: Started two weeks ago, taking as directed.

symptom_diary.entry: Started the new low-carb diet. Sugars have been great, mostly 110-130!
It was my niece's birthday — had cake and a glass of juice. Worth it!
Whoa, meter said 247 this morning. Must be the cake. Feel totally fine.

patient_concerns: Wonders if one high reading after a birthday means her diabetes is out of control.
